name: reaver
title: reaver
summary: brute force attack tool against Wi-Fi Protected Setup PIN number
description:
brute force attack tool against Wi-Fi Protected Setup PIN number
Reaver performs a brute force attack against an access point's Wi-Fi
Protected Setup pin number.
Once the WPS pin is found, the WPA PSK can be recovered and alternately
the AP's wireless settings can be reconfigured.
This package also provides the Wash executable, an utility for identifying WPS
enabled access points. See documentation in /usr/share/doc/reaver/README.WASH.

use_cases: When you need Kali Linux, use reaver because brute force attack tool against Wi-Fi Protected Setup PIN number.
When the engagement is in the install phase, use reaver as a cataloged Kali Linux tool with operator ACCEPT/CANCEL gating.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `reaver -i <interface> -b <target bssid> -vv` as documented upstream for reaver.
When chaining Kali Linux tools, pair reaver with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `reaver --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.
